stocks.recent_news: Berjis Desai resigned as Non-Executive, Non-Independent Director effective April 24, 2026. On April 6, 2026 contracted to sell 2007-built MR tanker 'Jag Prakash' (~47,848 dwt) - delivery in Q1 FY27 (fleet renewal). Q4 FY26 results scheduled for May 8, 2026. Q3 FY26 (Dec qtr) consolidated PAT was up 36.9% YoY to Rs 812.5 cr on +17.6% net sales of Rs 1,454 cr; 3rd interim dividend of Rs 9/share declared January 29, 2026. Stock around Rs 1,372-1,424 in early/mid-April 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Tanker market resilient into 2026 - supported by OPEC+ supply adjustments and elevated tonne-mile demand from longer voyages amid Middle East tensions. Aframax/Suezmax mid-size strength outpacing VLCCs. Headwind: large newbuild order book from 2024-25 raises FY27+ supply concerns; Indian Russian-crude flows declining on US/EU pressure shifting trade patterns.
stocks.peer_comparison: Best-in-class diversified Indian shipping operator (crude, product, dry bulk, offshore via Greatship India) with fleet of 80+ vessels including 27 tankers + 14 dry bulk carriers. Outperformed shipping-pure peers (SCI) on profitability through CY25. Q3 PAT growth (+36.9% YoY) leverages tanker cycle better than SCI.
red_flags: Tanker rate softening risk in FY27 from elevated newbuild deliveries
Aging tonnage (selling 2007-built MR tankers) means realised gain but capex cycle ahead
Re-routing of Russian crude flows could compress utilisation on key India routes
Director resignation (Berjis Desai) on April 24, 2026 - likely procedural
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results on May 8, 2026 (likely final + 4th interim dividend)
Asset-sale gain on Jag Prakash to flow through Q1 FY27 P&L
Tanker rate strength from Middle East geopolitical tensions

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results approved by board April 24, 2026: standalone net profit Rs 1,400 cr / consolidated Rs 1,421.85 cr - up ~203% YoY and ~42% QoQ; GRM surged to US$13.75/bbl vs US$6.22/bbl YoY. FY26 full-year PAT ~Rs 3,062-3,102 cr (+1,349% YoY) on revenue Rs 63,640-78,611 cr; FY26 GRM US$9.28/bbl (vs US$4.22). Crude throughput 11.71 MMT at 112% capacity utilisation (record). Final dividend of Rs 54/share recommended. V.C. Asokan appointed Non-Executive Nominee Director effective April 2, 2026 (IOCL ED, 30+ years O&G experience). CMP ~Rs 1,138.7 on April 30, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian refining strong on widening complex GRMs after Brent breached US$100/bbl in March 2026. Diesel cracks in US$50-60/bbl range. UBS downgraded HPCL/BPCL/IOC on marketing-margin squeeze, but pure refiners benefit unambiguously from elevated cracks. Q4 saw some West Asia conflict-driven crude volatility but full-year GRMs more than doubled industry-wide.
stocks.peer_comparison: As a pure refiner (no fuel retailing), CPCL is insulated from the marketing-margin squeeze hurting OMC peers (HPCL, BPCL, IOC). Q4 GRM US$13.75/bbl ahead of MRPL ($9.22 FY26) and HPCL Q3 US$8.9, roughly in line with BPCL Q3 US$13.3. 112% capacity utilisation is sector-best. CHENNPETRO FY26 PAT Rs 3,062 cr vs MRPL FY26 PAT Rs 1,931 cr.
red_flags: Crack spreads cyclical - sustainability of US$13.75 GRM uncertain if Brent normalises
Government windfall-tax / excise-duty risk reintroduction if oil prices stay elevated
Stock has rallied sharply on results - may have priced in much of the upside
catalysts: Rs 54/share final dividend record date pending
Continued elevated GRMs into Q1 FY27 if cracks hold
Parent IOC's Q4 results due (will reinforce or contradict refining theme)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 29, 2026 (street estimates: revenue Rs 44,000-46,000 cr; PAT Rs 3,000-3,600 cr - base effect from prior-year one-offs). Q3 FY26 had ARPU Rs 259 (+5.4% YoY) and PAT Rs 6,630 cr (-55% YoY on high base). Google broke ground on a US$15 bn Vizag AI hub (April 28) partnered with Nxtra by Airtel + AdaniConneX. DoT imposed small Rs 7.05 lakh penalty (April 25) for subscriber-verification norm violations. 3,400+ new 5G sites added in MH/Goa (April 21). Airtel Money received its NBFC license from RBI in February 2026. Stock at Rs 1,880.9 on April 29.
stocks.sector_context: Indian telecom in an upcycle on tariff hikes flowing through ARPU; sector-wide 5G monetisation building. Jefferies expects 15% across-the-board mobile tariff hike in June 2026 (~25% cumulative through FY27-28, ~16% ARPU CAGR). Each Rs 10 ARPU = ~Rs 2,500 cr EBITDA. AGR matter remains an industry-wide overhang.
stocks.peer_comparison: Industry leader on ARPU and post-paid mix; outperforming Vodafone Idea on subscriber additions and Reliance Jio on premium-mix and ARPU growth. Jefferies target Rs 2,760, JM Financial sees up to 37% upside. Best-positioned for upcoming tariff hikes given premium subscriber mix.
red_flags: Q3 PAT optically -55% YoY due to high base - risk of similar optics in Q4 numbers driving short-term volatility
DoT penalty on subscriber verification (small size but compliance signal)
Heavy 5G capex cycle continues
Stock under pressure broader market April 30 (Nifty opened down ~180 pts)
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results just out April 29 - ARPU print and subscriber adds key drivers
Final dividend declaration with Q4
Vizag AI data-centre partnership with Google (long-dated revenue catalyst for Nxtra)
Airtel Money NBFC scale-up
Expected tariff hike June-July 2026

stocks.recent_news: FY26 total sales hit record 51,17,667 units (+10% YoY); 2W +8% to 43,16,850, 3W +20% to 8,00,817. March 2026 standalone: 2W +21% YoY to 3,80,473 units, CV +20% YoY to 64,904 units. Bajaj WEGO electric 3-wheeler launched April 14, 2026 with class-leading 296 km range. Bajaj GoGo P7012 took #1 position in E-Auto segment in April. Exports +21% in FY26 to 22,50,183 units. Q4 FY26 results scheduled for May 6, 2026 - board to also consider interim dividend.
stocks.sector_context: India 2W market hit record 21.4M units in FY26, crossing pre-COVID peak. Industry retail sales in March 2026 were 22.66 lakh units (+18% YoY). TVS Motor fastest-growing major OEM (+23% YoY). Hero MotoCorp regained leadership over Honda (6.07M vs 5.75M units). Electric 2W segment up ~45% YoY in March (191K units), with TVS Chetak iQube and Bajaj Chetak as top players.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bajaj domestic 2W market share losing ground to TVS and Hero in core motorcycle segment per Q3 FY26 commentary. However, Bajaj #1 in 3W (both ICE and EV) and strong export franchise (~22% of FY26 sales). Premium valuation vs peers despite slower domestic 2W growth. ICICI Securities forecasts Q4 PAT Rs 2,613 cr (+27.5% YoY) and revenue Rs 15,714 cr (+29.4% YoY).
red_flags: Domestic 2W market share losing ground to TVS/Hero - Q3 FY26 highlighted concern
Premium valuation vs peers despite slower domestic 2W growth
Heavy reliance on exports (~22%) exposes to FX/global demand swings
Broader market under pressure April 30 - results-week profit booking risk
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results on May 6, 2026 (~6 days away) - consensus +27.5% YoY profit growth
Interim dividend likely to be announced with Q4
Strong March quarter with 20% sales growth feeds Q4 numbers
EV 3-wheeler leadership (#1 with GoGo P7012, WEGO 296 km range launch)
FY26 record sales of 51.17 lakh units sets up positive base

stocks.recent_news: Stock surged ~9% intraday on April 28, 2026 (close ~Rs 766-788) on exceptionally heavy volumes (5.2M shares vs 0.8M prior session). Up 12%+ over two consecutive sessions. Board meeting scheduled May 4, 2026 to approve Q4/FY26 results and consider dividend. UK structure simplification: Cheshire Salt Holdings and New Cheshire Salt Works dissolved April 14, 2026. Q3 FY26 net loss of Rs 69 cr with revenues -1.11% YoY to Rs 3,550 cr. Rs 515 cr greenfield IVSD facility planned at Valinokkam, Tamil Nadu.
stocks.sector_context: Global soda ash market in significant oversupply phase, pressuring prices. Energy prices volatile (key cost input). Recent China soda ash price hikes (~13% in 3 days, +6% in one day) provided sentiment boost. Government MIP (minimum import price) of Rs 20,108/tonne on soda ash provided some support but expired June 2025 - extension uncertain.
stocks.peer_comparison: GHCL is dominant domestic peer (1.2 mtpa, ~25% domestic demand) and trades at P/E ~8-9x vs TATACHEM ~45x - significant valuation gap. Both pressured by global oversupply but TATACHEM has larger international exposure (UK/US/Kenya operations). Stock down sharply from 52-week high of Rs 1,026.65 to ~Rs 633 in late March 2026 before recent bounce.
red_flags: Morgan Stanley issued double downgrade with ~50% price target cut
Q3 FY26 net loss of Rs 69 cr - profitability under pressure
Continuous global soda ash oversupply with high energy costs
P/E of ~45x vs GHCL ~8-9x suggests rich valuation despite weak earnings
Sharp 9% spike on heavy volumes ahead of results raises potential leak/insider activity concern
catalysts: Q4 FY26 board meeting May 4, 2026 - results and dividend recommendation
Recent rally suggests positive earnings positioning by smart money
Greenfield IVSD facility at Valinokkam (Rs 515 cr investment) for capacity addition
China soda ash price uptick may support sentiment
Possible MIP extension by government
